members.rationale: Peers confirm improvements. Member_D raised efficiency from 6 to 8, validating that the table condensation worked. Member_C's safety improvement (CRL/OCSP) doesn't affect my assessment but shows the corpus is filling gaps systematically. The remaining TESTING_SPEC.md parenthetical references are truly minor — they're in section headers where markdown links would be awkward. Maintaining 8.
No challenges from peers on capability coverage. All five members have scored their lens at 8+, indicating the corpus is now at a high-quality baseline across all dimensions. Maintaining 9.

Peer scores all at 8+. Member_A's coherence improvements (relative links) have no safety impact. Member_D's efficiency improvements removed no security content. The safety posture is strong: threat model, amplification, replay, DoS, timing, revocation all addressed. Maintaining 9.

Cross-review validates my score increase. The 146-line reduction is meaningful for a documentation corpus — it's roughly 2% of the total but targeted at the most verbose sections. The INDEX.md addition (66 lines) is justified by the navigability gain. Remaining opportunities: some research notes could cross-reference specs more tightly to avoid reader needing both open, but this is a minor style preference, not a material efficiency issue. Maintaining 8.

All peers at 8+ confirms the corpus has reached a quality plateau. The iterative council loop itself is demonstrating a valuable pattern: specification-as-living-document with structured quality gates. This meta-pattern is generalizable. Maintaining 9.
